title: Reverse transcription, primer pools preparation and multiplex PCR steps for CHIKV serotype  genomic sequencing
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"cueol","text":"This step-by-step protocol describes the cDNA synthesis, primer pools preparation and multiplex PCR conditions with the main goal to sequence the complete genome of CHIKV serotype strains.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"cj0p8","text":"Reverse transcription: SuperScript™ IV First-Strand Synthesis System. (200 reactions) Cat:18091200 Invitrogen","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"87s2s","text":"\nMultiplex PCR: Q5® High-Fidelity 2X Master Mix. Cat: M0492L NEB, H20 Ultre Pure, primers described in table 1.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Using a 2mL tube prepare the Mix 1 described below for 96 samples: A B C 1 Mix 1Reverse transcription Vol. (1x) 96 samples (+2 = 98 to keepsome extra due to pipettingissues) 2 Random Hexamers (50µM) 1µL 98µL 3 dNTPs mix (10mM each) 1µL 98µL 4 Total 2 µL 194µL

Using 0,2mL PCR tubes or 96 wells plates add 11-16µL of extracted RNA fromRT-PCR positive samples. Add 2µL of Mix 1 to the tube/well and take it to thethermocycler with the following set up: 65ºC ---- 5 minutes
Take the tubes/wells to ice for 1 minute. (you can prepare a water bath with ice cubes to have a uniform temperature distribution).

Using a 2mL tube prepare Mix 2 : A B C 1 Mix 2Reverse Transcription Vol.(1x) 96 samples (+2 = 98 to keepsome extra due to pipettingissues) 2 5x SSIV Buffer 4µL 392µL 3 100mM DTT 1µL 98µL 4 RNaseOUT orRNase Inhibitor 1µL 98µL 5 SSIV Reverse Transcriptase 1µL 98µL 6 Total 7µL 686µL
Add 7µL of Mix 2 to the tubes containing the Mix 1 plus RNA and take it to the thermocycler following the set up below: Step1:42ºC ---- 50 minutes70ºC ---- 10 minutes4ºC ---- Hold

Store the cDNA at -20ºC. Observation:. As a suggestion, to improve the final results only samples RT-PCR positive showing a Ct value of < 30 should be used for cDNA conversion and genomic amplification.
Select two 0,6mL tubes for each pool.
Using the original 100uM primer solution eluted individually, put them together following the table below containing each primer volume.
Pool 1 will have a final volume of 469µl and pool 2 of 460µl.

In order to prepare the solution to use in the Multiplex PCR, dilute each pool 1:10. That is, 10µl of pool 1 and 90µl of ultrapure water. in the 25µl PCR reaction. Note: The primers were designed using the https://primalscheme.com based on theKP164576, KP164571, KP164572, KP164568, KP164570 and KP164569 reference genomes (Machado, 2019).

Prepare the Mix 1 for a Multiplex PCR for each Pool 1 and Pool 2 using a Falcon tubeof 15mL (~96 amostras) or a 2mL tube. A B C D 1 Mix 1Multiplex PCR Vol. Pool 1(1x) Vol. Pool 2(1x) 96 samples (+2) (pool1 ou pool2) 2 Q5 Master Mix Highfidelity 2X 12,5 µl 12,5 µl 1.225 µl 3 Pool primers (Pool1 orPool2) /Use concentration 1,7 µl 1,7 µl 166,6 µl 4 Ultra Pure Water 8,3 µl 8,3 µl 813,3 µl 5 Total 22,5µl 22,5µl 2205µl

Add 2,5µl of cDNA (totalling 5µl) in 22,5µl of the pool1 and pool2 reaction and takeit to the thermocycler following the conditions bellow: Step1:98ºC ---- 30 secondsStep2: (45 cycles)98ºC ---- 15 seconds58ºC ---- 30 seconds72ºC ---- 5 minutesStep3: 72ºC ---- 2 minutesHold 4ºC
